summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that EAP session identifiers are generated using a predictable PRNG seeded with Unix timestamps, enabling an attacker to predict the identifier and craft valid session confirmation requests.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction demonstrates that an attacker can compute the EAP identifier by knowing the approximate timestamp, enabling session hijacking and authentication bypass in EAP-AKA' flows.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction verified the vulnerable code pattern (math/rand seeded with time.Now().UnixNano()) exists in the target repository, and demonstrated that the same timestamp consistently produces the same EAP identifier. The search space was confirmed to be 256 possible values (uint8 range), and the deterministic relationship between timestamp and identifier was validated across multiple trials.
verification_method: The reproduction performs two verification steps: (1) static code analysis confirms the vulnerable PRNG pattern (rand.NewSource(time.Now().UnixNano()) + Intn(256)) exists in ue_authentication.go, and (2) a runtime demonstration proves that identical timestamps produce identical identifiers, confirming the PRNG is deterministic and predictable.
evidence_produced_by_run: Vulnerable code pattern rand.NewSource(time.Now().UnixNano()) confirmed in ue_authentication.go
Intn(256) pattern confirmed, limiting search space to 256 possible values
Same timestamp produces identical EAP identifiers across multiple runs
Attack feasibility demonstrated: attacker can predict identifier within nanosecond-level time windows

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the main branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
